title: Slow-cooker chilli con carne
subtitle: Beef mince, kidney beans, and tomato, low and slow
excerpt: Beef mince cooked with kidney beans, tinned tomatoes, chilli powder, and cumin in the slow cooker for six to eight hours until thick, deep, and properly spiced. The long cook melds the flavours in a way a quick stovetop version cannot. Serve with rice, jacket potatoes, or crusty bread.
sourceNotes: Chilli con carne arrived in British kitchens in the 1970s and quickly became one of the most cooked dishes in the country. The slow cooker version became standard shortly after — it suits the long, low method that improves ground meat dishes. The British version is less fiery than Tex-Mex originals, uses canned kidney beans, and is typically served with rice.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes extremely well. A batch cook staple.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe — this freezes perfectly and the slow cooker handles the extra volume.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the next day. The spice mellows and the sauce thickens as it rests.

content.text: Brown the mince before it goes into the slow cooker. Raw mince dropped directly into the pot cooks into grey, clumped lumps rather than breaking down into the sauce. Five minutes in a hot pan, breaking it up thoroughly, changes the final texture completely.

items.prepNote: 70%, optional, stirred in at the end

content.text: Heat a wide pan over high heat with a splash of oil. Fry the {{onion}} for 5 minutes until softened. Add the {{beef-mince}} and break it up thoroughly, cooking until browned all over with no pink remaining, about 8 minutes. Add the {{garlic}}, {{chilli-powder}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, and {{tomato-puree}} and cook for 1 minute. Tip everything into the slow cooker. Add the {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{kidney-beans}}, {{stock-beef}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Cook on low for 6 to 8 hours. Stir in the {{dark-chocolate}} in the last 5 minutes if using — it rounds the flavour without tasting of chocolate. Taste and adjust seasoning before serving.

items.cause: Tinned tomatoes released a lot of liquid, or lid not sealing
items.fix: Remove the lid and switch to high for the last 30 to 45 minutes to reduce. For a thicker result from the start, use only half the stock

items.symptom: Mince in grey clumps rather than broken down
items.cause: Not browned before going into the slow cooker
items.fix: Always brown the mince thoroughly in a hot pan, breaking it up completely, before adding it. This step cannot be skipped without affecting the texture

items.cause: UK chilli powder is pure ground chilli — much hotter than US "chili powder" blend
items.fix: Start with 1 teaspoon and add more once tasted at the end. The spice builds over the long cook

content.text: Chilli con carne is among the most batch-cooked and frozen dishes in the British household. The slow cooker version is built in the morning and requires no attention until dinner. The dark chocolate at the end is not traditional but rounds the acidity from the tomatoes — use it or skip it. Serve over rice, with soured cream, grated cheddar, and fresh coriander for those who want it.
